error_signature: No binary data found in input. Check previous node output.
title: Binary data undefined in Code node after upload
symptom: After uploading an audio file via a Webhook node, the subsequent Code node throws an error stating 'No binary data found in input'. This indicates that the expected binary data is not available.
applies_when: Using Code node to process binary data
Webhook node successfully uploads binary data
not_this_if: Binary data is present in the previous node output
Different file formats are being used
root_cause: The issue arises when the second Code node incorrectly modifies the MIME type of the binary data. If the MIME type does not match the expected format, the subsequent Code node cannot find the binary data, leading to the error.
fix_steps: Remove any code that modifies the MIME type of the binary data in the second Code node.
Ensure that the correct audio file format is being uploaded and passed through the workflow.
agent_instruction: Check the MIME type handling in the Code node and ensure it matches the uploaded audio file format.
